chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS C.P No.D-56 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection (s) 2. For hearing of main case 28.01.2026 Mr.Bhooro Bheel, Advocate for the petitioner Mr.Rafiq Ahmed Dahri, Assistant A.G Sindh a/w SSP Umerkot (Uazir Ahmed), SHO PS City Umerkot (Insp. Ahmed), Secretary DRTA, Umkerkot (Syed Shiraz Ali Shah) and Mukhtiarkar, Umerkot (Abrar Ahmed) ********** The para‐wise comments filed on behalf of respondents No.3, 8, 9 and 10 are taken on record. Copies thereof have been supplied to learned counsel for the petitioner. Mr. Sandeep Kumar Maheshwari, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondent No.4 and Mr. Aziz Ahmed Laghari, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondent No.12. The same are taken on record. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that respondents No.8 and 9, who are police officials, have been unlawfully harassing the petitioner and interfering in the operation of his bus terminal, operating under the name “Hur Air Conditioned Coach Service” at Umerkot City. It is further alleged that the said bus stand has been sealed by the police without lawful authority. Upon confrontation, the S.S.P. Umerkot categorically denies the allegations. He submits that neither the petitioner has been harassed nor has the bus stand been sealed by the police. He explains that police mobiles were deployed in the vicinity solely to maintain law and order due to an ongoing dispute between two private transporters. He further undertakes before this Court that the police shall not harass the petitioner, shall not interfere in his business and shall act strictly in accordance with law.

He explains that police mobiles were deployed in the vicinity solely to maintain law and order due to an ongoing dispute between two private transporters. He further undertakes before this Court that the police shall not harass the petitioner, shall not interfere in his business and shall act strictly in accordance with law. 2 Learned counsel for the petitioner further submits that the petitioner has already moved an application before the Regional Transport Authority (RTA), Umerkot, which remains pending and undecided. The Secretary, DRTA, who is present in Court, acknowledges the pendency of the said application and undertakes that the same shall be decided strictly in accordance with law, after providing due opportunity of hearing to the petitioner, within one week from today. In view of the statements and undertakings recorded hereinabove, the petition stands disposed of accordingly. Compliance report shall be submitted to the Additional Registrar of this Court, by the concerned R.T.O within fifteen days. JUDGE JUDGE AHSAN K. ABRO
